findings.evidence: Line 79 states `legendreSym q (oddPrimeDiscriminant p) = 1 ↔ legendreSym p (q : ℤ) = 1`; roadmap lines 97-100 specify the splitting API in exactly the `legendreSym p dⱼ = 1` normal form.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `legendreSym_oddPrimeDiscriminant_eq_one_iff` (or to the pointwise equality lemma if that is the intended simp normal form) and keep the family wrapper derivable from it.
findings.issue: The characteristic residue-condition rewrite is not a simp lemma, so the roadmap target shape `∀ i, legendreSym p dⱼ = 1` will not normalize from `oddPrimeDiscriminant` radicands by automation.

findings.evidence: Line 86 states the core iff without an attribute; line 95 marks only `not_dvd_oddPrimeDiscriminant_iff` as `[simp]`, while existing `PrimeDiscriminant.lean:89` has `@[simp] theorem oddPrimeDiscriminant_dvd_iff`.
findings.fix: Mark `dvd_oddPrimeDiscriminant_iff` with `@[simp]` so `(q : ℤ) ∣ oddPrimeDiscriminant p` normalizes to `(q : ℤ) ∣ (p : ℤ)`.
findings.issue: The positive divisibility normal form for unramifiedness is public but not marked `[simp]`, unlike the adjacent negated form and the existing divisibility API.

summary: The exposed lemmas characterize the intended odd-prime-discriminant Legendre API, but two normal-form lemmas are missing the automation annotations this public surface needs.
